name: Proxy
def: A stand-in that controls access to another object, adding behaviour around it transparently.
problem: Intercept access for lazy loading, caching, access control, or remote calls without changing the client.
mechanism: The proxy implements the same interface as the real subject and forwards calls, adding logic (virtual, protection, remote, caching proxies).
failures: Surprising performance from hidden remote/lazy proxies
whenNot: When direct access has no cross-cutting concern
myths: "Proxy and Decorator are interchangeable" — proxy controls access; decorator adds behaviour
